title: An apparatus for manufacturing a composite component
abstract: There is disclosed an apparatus (10) for manufacturing a composite component, comprising: a first tool (12) having a first support surface (22) for engaging a pre-form (100) for the composite component; a second tool (16) having a second support surface (46) for engaging the pre-form (100); a carrier (14) for the second tool (16) having an aperture (26) for withdrawal of the composite component (100) from the apparatus (10), wherein the carrier (14) is configured to receive the second tool (16) in a closed configuration in which the second tool (16) closes the aperture (26), and wherein the second tool (16) is moveable relative the carrier (14) to reveal the aperture (26); and a flexible seal (34) between the first tool (12) and the carrier (14). The first tool (12) and the carrier (14) are configured for relative movement so that, with the second tool (16) in the closed configuration, the first and second support surfaces (22, 46) can be selectively engaged and disengaged around the pre-form. A corresponding method of curing a composite component is also disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . An apparatus for manufacturing a composite component, comprising: a first tool having a first support surface for engaging a pre-form for the composite component; a second tool having a second support surface for engaging the pre-form; a carrier for the second tool having an aperture for withdrawal of the composite component from the apparatus, wherein the carrier is configured to receive the second tool in a closed configuration in which the second tool closes the aperture, and wherein the second tool is moveable relative the carrier to reveal the aperture; and a flexible seal between the first tool and the carrier; wherein the first tool and the carrier are configured for relative movement so that, with the second tool in the closed configuration, the first and second support surfaces can be selectively engaged and disengaged around the pre-form; and wherein the flexible seal is configured to expand and/or contracts to accommodate relative movement between the first tool and the carrier between the disengaged and engaged configurations, such that in use with the second tool in the closed configuration, an interior space between the first tool and second tool is sealed from the external environment. 2. An apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the first tool and the carrier are configured to move relatively closer together from a disengaged configuration to an engaged configuration. 3. An apparatus according to claim 2, further comprising a stop configured to prevent the first tool and the carrier moving relatively closer together beyond the engaged configuration. 4. An apparatus according to any preceding claim, further comprising an aperture seal arranged to provide a gas-tight seal between the carrier and the second tool in the closed configuration. 5. An apparatus according to claim 4, wherein the aperture seal comprises an O- ring. 6. An apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the flexible seal is configured to provide a gas-tight seal between the carrier and the first tool such that, in use with the second tool in the closed configuration, an interior space between the first tool and second tool is sealed from the external environment. 7. An apparatus according to any preceding claim, further comprising an extraction port for extracting gas from the apparatus. 8. An apparatus according to claim 7, further comprising a low pressure or vacuum source coupled to the extraction port. 9. An apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the first tool is static and the carrier is configured to move with respect to the first tool. 10. An apparatus according to any preceding claim, wherein the first tool and the carrier are configured for relative movement along a longitudinal direction. 1 1 . An apparatus according to claim 10, further comprising an alignment mechanism configured to prevent lateral relative movement between the first tool and the carrier. 12. An apparatus according to claim 1 1 , wherein the alignment mechanism comprises an alignment member configured to cooperate with corresponding portions of the first tool and the carrier to prevent lateral relative movement between the first tool and the carrier. 13. An apparatus according to claim 12, wherein the alignment member is resiliently compressible, and is configured to be compressed as the carrier moves relatively towards the first tool. 14. An apparatus according to claim 13 when appendant to claim 2, wherein the alignment member is configured to bias the first tool and carrier to a disengaged configuration. 15. An apparatus according to any preceding claim, further comprising a carrier alignment mechanism configured so that the second tool is constrained to be received in the closed configuration in a predetermined orientation. 16. An apparatus according to claim 14 or 15 when appendant to claim 4, wherein the carrier alignment mechanism is disposed outside of an interior space of the apparatus at least partly defined by the aperture seal when the second tool is in the closed configuration. 17. An apparatus according to any preceding claim, further comprising a heat transfer portion formed in or attached to an outer surface of one of the first tool, the second tool and the carrier. 18. An apparatus for manufacturing a composite fan blade in accordance with any preceding claim, wherein the first support surface and the second support surface correspond to opposing surfaces of an aerofoil portion of the fan blade. 19. A method of curing a composite component comprising : providing a pre-form for the composite component on a first support surface of a first tool; causing relative movement between a carrier and the first tool so that a second tool received on the carrier moves from a disengaged configuration to an engaged configuration in which a second support surface of the second tool engages the preform; causing the pre-form to cure, including fluidically coupling a sealed space in which the pre-form is disposed to a low pressure or vacuum source, wherein the sealed space is at least partly defined by a flexible seal between the first tool and the carrier; causing the second tool to move relative the carrier from a closed configuration in which an aperture of the carrier is closed to an open configuration to reveal the aperture; and removing the cured composite component from the first tool through the aperture; wherein the flexible seal expands and/or contracts to accommodate relative movement between the first tool and carrier between the disengaged and engaged configurations, such that in use with the second tool in the closed configuration, an 

 interior space between the first tool and second tool is sealed from the external environment.. 20. A method according to claim 19, further comprising placing or applying fibre reinforcement material on the first tool through the aperture before the second tool is received on the carrier. 21 . A method according to claim 19 or 20, further comprising moving the second tool relative the carrier from an open configuration to a closed configuration so that the second tool is received on the carrier to sealingly close the aperture, before causing relative movement between the carrier and the first tool from the disengaged configuration to the engaged configuration. 22. A method according to any of claims 19 to 21 , further comprising extracting gas from an interior space defined between the first tool and the carrier through an extraction port. 23. A method according to any of claims 19 to 22, wherein the carrier moves between the disengaged and engaged configurations whilst the first tool remains substantially stationary. 24. A method according to any of claims 19 to 23, wherein the relative movement between the first tool and the carrier is along a longitudinal direction. 25. A method of curing a fan blade for a gas turbine engine in accordance with any of claims 19 to 24. 26. A method of curing a plurality of composite components comprising conducting a method in accordance with any of claims 19 to 25 successive times to cure each composite component, wherein the same flexible seal between the first tool and the carrier is used for each successive composite component.
